title: Fix CLI download chunk lookup and timeout warning
description: Investigate the CLI download failure for a real UFID where the file card is found but chunk messages are reported missing/malformed, and address the TimeoutNegativeWarning seen during the same download attempt.
close_reason: Fixed CLI download message fetch wait-time handling, accepted GramJS integer document sizes, and improved invalid file-card diagnostics for broken chunk references.

title: Fix PWA share target upload flow
close_reason: Closed

title: Bump tglfs CLI to 0.1.1 for npm release
description: Prepare the tglfs npm package for a 0.1.1 release from main by updating the package version, verifying lockfile metadata, and running build/test/pack before publish.
acceptance_criteria:
1. packages/tglfs-cli/package.json is version 0.1.1.
2. Relevant package-lock metadata matches 0.1.1.
3. Build, tests, and npm pack succeed on main.
4. If npm auth is unavailable, report the exact blocker instead of publishing.
close_reason: Bumped tglfs CLI to 0.1.1, updated release metadata, and verified build/test/pack. Actual npm publish is blocked by invalid npm auth and tracked separately in tglfs-7ug.

title: Improve login user experience
description: Improve the login experience with an inline, guided flow for phone, code, and optional password steps. Replace blocking prompts with status messaging, error handling, and clear feedback while keeping styling consistent with the existing UI.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add PWA share target support for uploads
description: Investigate and implement Web Share Target for the PWA so shared files can be received and piped into existing upload flow.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add diagnostics or repair tooling for corrupted TGLFS file cards
description: Some existing TGLFS file cards in Saved Messages reference chunk IDs that no longer point at Telegram document messages. The CLI now reports this precisely, but there is no tool to inspect or repair the broken metadata. Add a follow-up utility or documented recovery workflow for these corrupted file cards.

title: Abort preview decompression when media append fails
description: Streaming preview can stop reading decompressed media after a SourceBuffer append failure, leaving the producer side blocked on backpressure instead of failing fast. Abort the decompression writer when append/read side fails so preview errors surface promptly for webm/mp4 playback.
close_reason: Abort preview decompression writes when media append or reader consumption fails so playback errors surface instead of stalling behind backpressure.

title: Fix share target when app opens without upload
description: Share target opens the PWA from Android gallery but upload doesn't start. Improve SW handling for non-File form entries and request share data even without query param, retrying after controller change.
close_reason: Closed

title: Prevent SW install failure when offline page missing
description: Service worker update fails on prod because caching /offline.html 404s. Ignore cache.add failures so SW still installs and share target works.
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix share target DB store and form parsing
description: Share target share DB missing store after debug snippet and files not parsed when keys differ. Bump share DB version and parse all formData values.
close_reason: Closed

title: Suppress GramJS localStorage warning when importing web Telegram helpers under Node
description: During live CLI/web parity smoke tests, importing src/telegram.ts directly under Node/tsx emitted `(node) Warning: --localstorage-file was provided without a valid path`. The CLI runtime already avoids this via a localStorage shim; investigate whether the shared/web-side Telegram helper can avoid the same warning in Node-based smoke scripts without affecting browser behavior.
close_reason: Replaced the web-side static GramJS imports with a shared Node-safe loader and added a regression test that imports src/telegram.ts under Node without emitting the localstorage-file warning.

title: Reduce duplicated protocol logic between the web app and TGLFS CLI
description: Audit the new CLI and existing web app for duplicated UFID, crypto, and file-card protocol logic and extract a safe shared core where doing so will not re-couple the CLI to browser-only modules.

title: Publish tglfs 0.1.1 to npm after refreshing npm auth
description: The tglfs package version has been bumped to 0.1.1 on main and release checks passed, but npm publish is blocked because the current npm auth/token cannot publish the existing tglfs package owned by xnil. Refresh npm login/token for the owning account and publish 0.1.1 from main.
acceptance_criteria:
1. `npm whoami` succeeds for the owning npm account.
2. `npm publish --workspace tglfs` succeeds from main.
3. `npm view tglfs version` returns 0.1.1.

title: Add CLI search with shared file-card search core
description: Implement `tglfs search [query]` with limit/offset-id/sort/json support, shared file-card parsing/search helpers reused by the web app and CLI, terminal table output, manpage/help/docs updates, and regression tests for both the shared core and CLI surface.
close_reason: Added `tglfs search` with limit/offset-id/sort/json support, introduced a shared file-card/search core reused by the web app and CLI, updated help/man/docs, and validated the command against a real saved Telegram session and packed CLI tarball.

title: Merge TGLFS CLI work to main and prepare npm release metadata
description: Merge the codex/tglfs-cli branch into main, add the remaining npm package metadata needed for publication, run release-quality gates on main, and leave the repo ready for a later `npm publish` from main once an npm account is available.
close_reason: Merged codex/tglfs-cli into main, added npm publication metadata to the CLI package, and verified test/build/pack on main so the repository is ready for a later npm publish from main.

title: Ensure share target files load reliably on production
description: Share target opens app but upload does not start on production PWA; add robust SW update + share fetch retry to prevent missed data.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add CLI download progress reporting
description: Show human-readable progress while `tglfs download` is fetching, decrypting, and writing a file, without breaking `--json` output or non-interactive automation flows.
close_reason: Added TTY-only download progress reporting, wired progress callbacks through the restore pipeline, documented the behavior, and covered it with renderer/pipeline tests.

title: Add browser MP4/MOV remux prompt
close_reason: Implemented browser-only MP4/MOV remux prompt using lazy-loaded FFmpeg WASM, with MOV-to-MP4 naming, remux failure alerts, web build, CLI build, and CLI tests passing.

title: Harden tglfs CLI automation/non-TTY behavior for JSON and prompt handling
description: Fix false-success and silent-output behavior in automation/non-TTY contexts. --json must force non-interactive execution, never invoke prompts, print concrete JSON on success to stdout, print visible/structured errors to stderr on failure, and return nonzero on missing input, prompt cancellation, or unanswered prompts. Upload without an explicit password source should default to unencrypted upload without prompting.
acceptance_criteria:
1. `--json` never invokes prompts.
2. Success in `--json` always emits stdout JSON and exits 0 only after completion.
3. Failure in `--json` emits an error on stderr and exits nonzero.
4. Upload without password flag/env/stdin defaults to empty password without prompting.
5. Prompt cancellation/unanswered/non-renderable states return nonzero.
6. Automated tests cover non-TTY subprocess behavior for status/search/upload JSON paths.
close_reason: Implemented strict JSON/non-interactive behavior, upload password defaulting, prompt cancellation handling, docs updates, and subprocess regression tests.

title: Add CLI upload, mutation, peer-transfer, legacy download, and inspect parity
description: Implement current-format upload and owned-file management commands (`upload`, `rename`, `delete`, `send`), extend search with `--peer`, add peer mailbox commands (`receive`, `unsend`), add `download --legacy` and `inspect`, and expand the shared protocol core so the web app and CLI reuse mutation, transfer, and upload/download semantics.
close_reason: Implemented CLI upload, rename/delete/send/receive/unsend, peer-aware search, legacy download, inspect, shared archive/Telegram helpers, docs/manpages, and automated coverage. Remaining live Telegram smoke testing is tracked separately in tglfs-mzv.

title: Fix npm ci/build dependency lock mismatch
description: npm ci fails because package-lock.json is out of sync with package.json (picocolors mismatch). Update lockfile/deps so npm ci and parcel build work.
close_reason: Closed

title: Ignore or clean up beads sync runtime artifacts
description: Running `bd sync` in this repo currently leaves `.beads/.sync.lock` and `.beads/sync_base.jsonl` behind as untracked files even after a successful sync. Add ignore rules or cleanup handling so sync does not dirty the worktree with runtime artifacts.
close_reason: Added .beads ignore rules for .sync.lock and sync_base.jsonl and verified git now ignores both runtime artifacts after bd sync runs.

title: Run live CLI/web parity smoke tests
description: Use real Telegram accounts and existing web-uploaded files to manually verify CLI upload, rename, delete, send, receive, unsend, search --peer, download --legacy, and inspect across both the CLI and web app.
close_reason: Completed live CLI/web parity smoke tests, verified legacy inspect fallback fix against real Telegram, and cleaned up test artifacts.

title: Fix CLI login timeout/logging cleanup
description: Adjust the CLI login prompt to label the Telegram 2FA hint clearly and fix the post-login TIMEOUT/unhandled update-loop noise observed after successful auth, including any related session/logging cleanup in the CLI auth flow.
close_reason: Updated CLI auth wording to label the 2FA hint explicitly and refactored GramJS loading/teardown so the CLI avoids the Node localStorage warning and uses destroy() for clean shutdown after auth/session operations.

title: Clarify Telegram peer resolution in CLI help and docs
description: Update CLI help text, manpages, and README so send/receive/unsend explain how peer strings are resolved in Telegram/GramJS, what forms usually work (`username`, `@username`, `me`, resolvable contact phone numbers), and that `source` refers to the mailbox being searched rather than necessarily the original uploader.
close_reason: Updated CLI help text, README, and send/receive/unsend manpages to explain Telegram peer resolution and clarify that receive/unsend source values identify the mailbox being searched rather than necessarily the original uploader.

title: Refine inspect probing and delete confirmations in the CLI
description: Make `tglfs inspect` clearly communicate that it performs a full format probe rather than looking like an unexpected download, and make `tglfs delete` show the file details being confirmed before deletion.
close_reason: Made inspect lightweight by default with an explicit --probe flag for the full integrity check, improved delete confirmations to show file details, updated docs/help, and added regression tests.

title: Fix interactive CLI menu command dispatch
description: Running `tglfs` with no subcommand enters the prompts-based menu, but selecting an action currently dispatches Commander with argv shaped like `node tglfs ...` under `from: user`, which causes errors such as `unknown command 'node'`. Update the menu flow so selections invoke the intended subcommands intrinsically and add regression coverage.
close_reason: Fixed the interactive menu to dispatch Commander with true user-arg argv, added a shared helper for menu dispatch/splitting, and covered the regression with tests.

title: Resolve npm audit vulnerabilities and term-size deprecation
description: Investigate and address npm audit vulnerabilities and the term-size deprecation warning; update deps or tooling as needed.
notes: Migrated build tooling from Parcel to Vite 7.3.1; npm audit now reports 0 vulnerabilities. Added Vite config with polyfill shims, moved manifest/icons/service-worker into public, and updated app entry/README to match.
close_reason: Completed

title: Implement npm CLI for auth and UFID downloads
description: Implement a separate global npm CLI in this repo for persistent auth and UFID-based downloads of current-format TGLFS files. Keep CLI code isolated from the existing web app, add standard help/manpages, agent-usable help output, tests, and packaging support.
close_reason: Implemented a separate npm CLI package for persistent auth and current-format UFID downloads, including prompts-based UX, help/manpages, tests, and packaging verification.

title: Add logout button to main menu
description: Add a logout capability via a button on the main menu so users can clear their session and return to the login screen.
close_reason: Closed

title: Default CLI login to bundled Telegram API credentials
description: Make the new TGLFS CLI use the same built-in Telegram API ID and API hash as the web app by default, while still allowing explicit flag/env overrides and documenting the fallback behavior.
close_reason: Updated the CLI to default login to the same bundled Telegram API ID/hash as the web app, with overrides still available via flags/env and docs/tests updated accordingly.

title: Add upload and file management commands to the TGLFS CLI
description: Extend the new CLI beyond auth and UFID downloads with upload, list/search, rename, delete, and related file-management commands while preserving web/CLI protocol compatibility.
close_reason: Upload and file-management commands are already implemented in the CLI, including upload, search, rename, delete, send, receive, unsend, and related Telegram parity flows.

title: Fix symlinked npm CLI entrypoint detection in tglfs
description: The ESM CLI entrypoint guard compares import.meta.url to pathToFileURL(process.argv[1]).href, which fails for symlinked npm-installed binaries because process.argv[1] is the bin symlink while import.meta.url resolves to the real dist/cli.js path. The CLI then exits 0 with no output. Make entrypoint detection robust to symlinks and add regression coverage for symlinked execution.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Symlinked CLI invocation executes the entrypoint and prints normal output.
2. Direct invocation still works.
3. JSON mode works through the symlinked path.
4. Automated regression covers symlinked entrypoint execution.
close_reason: Fixed symlinked CLI entrypoint detection, added regression coverage, verified direct and npm-symlinked execution, and bumped the package version to 0.1.2.

title: Fix large encrypted downloads hanging mid-stream
close_reason: Replaced fixed-size decompression draining with a streaming service-worker pump in all download paths.
